name: tour-review
description: Review the tour proposal (breadth then depth) and fix findings until no Blocker or Major remains.
rubric: The breadth pass scanned the current draft for missing sections, unpriced line items, and unsupported marketing claims.
The depth pass returned an explicit verdict for each of the five gates (Logistics Feasibility, Pricing Reconciliation, Claim Substantiation, Safety & Compliance, Client Fit).
Zero Blocker findings remain (e.g., an infeasible same-day transfer, a total that does not reconcile, a missing cancellation policy).
Zero Major findings remain (e.g., a marketing claim the proposal cannot substantiate).
The final package is consistent with the stated segment, budget, and accessibility needs.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) breadth-review the current proposal for completeness and internal consistency; (2) depth-review by issuing a verdict on each gate in gates.md; (3) if no Blocker/Major findings remain and tour-validate is green, converge — report Minor/Nit findings without iterating; (4) otherwise fix the Blocker/Major findings in the proposal and re-run tour-validate before the next pass.
guardrails: Findings are fixed in the proposal, not argued away — a gate failure means the itinerary or pricing changes, not the verdict text.
Do not soften a safety term or drop the cancellation policy to clear a gate.
Re-review the fixes themselves each iteration, not just the prior findings list.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the remaining Blocker/Major findings, the fix attempted for each, and recommend human operator review or a return to the Plan stage.
